comments.text: The petition to fling Brandon Beane into Lake Erie via trebuchet has 80,000 signatures

Tomorrow is the 53rd anniversary of United States vs. Reserve Mining Company, filed by the DOJ on behalf of the EPA that stopped the dumping of taconite mining tailings into Lake Superior. Ever drank a glass of tap water in Duluth? That case is why you’re able to do so without drinking asbestos.

Coming back to update you all WE SAVED THE PORKIES!!! There will be no Copperwood mining around the Great Lakes. If you signed the petition or called the Michigan State Senate YOU helped to save over 1M ppl's water supply, prevented animal displacement and habitat loss. Thank you all SO MUCH!

BREAKING: Victory for Lake Superior! MN DNR suspends PolyMet copper-sulfide mine permit review until August 2025 -finally acknowledging that this dangerous project is fundamentally flawed (after spending millions of taxpayer dollars defending its shoddy permits). Time to move on from this project!

Good! This would have been a farce, $50 million to
benefit a Canadian company denuding our environment near Lake Superior. $50M for controversial UP copper mine on chopping block in Michigan budget www.myupnow.com/news/50m-for...
Nickel bearing sulfide vein and hand sample at one of the #Sudbury deposits, Canada. Institute on Lake Superior Geology field conference in 2022. #Minerals

A bit of good news: the Copperwood Mine grant that would have had terrible environmental impacts on the Porcupine Mountains and Lake Superior has been defeated! (For now -- stay vigilant. )

Please boost if you know ANYONE in Michigan. A foreign company with a bad track record might be awarded $50 million in taxpayer dollars to open a dangerous copper mine along Lake Superior. Which could destroy one of the largest sources of fresh water in the world.

In 90 days, the Minnesota Supreme Court will decide whether a mining company is required to fully examine how their proposed expansion will impact Lake Superior. Here’s what to know: greatlakesnow.org/2024/11/breaking-point-minnesotas-mining-legacy-includes-massive-lake-of-wastewater/

🧵 1/7 Toxic sands are threatening Buffalo Reef in Lake Superior, a critical spawning ground for lake trout and whitefish. This slow-moving disaster stems from century-old copper mining. What's being done about it? 🔗:
Sign this petition to protect the Porkies and Lake Superior! Please look, please sign & share and thank you for taking the time. 💚🌲 www.change.org/p/protect-th...

BIG NEWS!! The Michigan Senate took out the Copperwood Mining Grant from the bundle and didn't approve it! There's more that could happen via meetings and future attempts, but this is a huge win for the Porcupine Mountains and Lake Superior 💚💚💚

I want to cry. One fucking vote. They’re going to destroy some of the most pristine land in this country and fucking Lake Superior for a little bit of fucking copper. Every single person who voted for this should have to swim, bathe, and drink the runoff from these mines.

The Michigan House GOP budget includes $50 million for the Copperwood Mine project, which environmental advocates say could result in the pollution of Lake Superior with mining waste, Michigan Advance reports: planetdetroit.org/2025/09/high...

Contentious Copper-Nickel Mine Gets Permit Approval from Minnesota Appeals Court [Jan. 3, 2024] Environmental groups and the Fond du Lac Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Indians filed a lawsuit to overturn the permit. unicornriot.ninja/2024/content...

A proposed copper mine right on the shore of Lake Superior, which will leach waste into the lake--if the tailings don't simply flood into the water unchecked. This is an utterly evil endeavor. Sign the petition & boost please!

Not looking forward to dow poisoning the Mississippi headwaters or BP upending their vats of cadmium into the great lakes, not for any explicit profit motive but just the love of the game

All copper mines experience spills and accidental releases. Email your senators to tell them to vote NO on HJ140 to protect the water system that so many flora and fauna rely on for survival—including humans. It’s also WAY too close to Lake Superior for comfort.

The Copperwood Mine is a plan to mine next to & beneath Porcupine Mountains State Park in MI, ship the copper out of country, board up shop in <11 years, and leave behind 30+ million tons of mining waste dangerously close to Lake Superior. .bsky.social www.change.org/p/protect-th...

The Michigan House voted yesterday to give a $50m grant to dump mining waste upstream from Lake Superior. It was unpopular, but they pushed it through by bundling it with larger grants for auto and AI It could still fail in the Senate, thankfully.

Trump put the NorthMet mine proposal for a massive open pit copper-nickel mine near Hoyt Lakes MN, on a priority list for speedier permits. Friends of the Boundary Waters have been fighting to block it, because toxic mine waste may flow towards Lake Superior. www.startribune.com/trump-names-...

The clock is ticking in a legal battle over a Minnesota mining project that campaigners warn could inflict catastrophic damage on Lake Superior and nearby communities. Officials argue a 50-year-old environmental assessment shows proposed works at the site are safe. www.newsweek.com/lake-superio...

Highland Copper Company wants to build a sulfide copper mine near Lake Superior which holds 10% of the world’s freshwater. The mine would produce 98.5% toxic waste, stored two miles from the lake. These mines have led to long-term environmental damage, all have failed to prevent pollution.
Do you love Lake Superior, home of 10% of the world's fresh surface water? I do! Sign the petition to stop the Copperwood Mine. #michigan #protecttheporkies

Michigan, Minnesota and Canadian friends. Please help me spread the word about the upcoming protest against the Copperwood mine on the shores of Lake Superior. If you value the pristine fresh water of Lake Superior and the Porcupine mountains, please join us. Toxic waste storage doesn't belong here

They want to open a copper mine in one of the last remaining old growth forests in North America, the Porcupine Mountain region in the Upper Peninsula of Michigan. This could end up contaminating the Great Lakes.

The Empire and Tilden mines that Trump gave exemptions to are in the Upper Peninsula of Michigan and impact the water table, which feeds into Lake Superior. The Great Lakes are all interconnected. If one gets polluted from mining waste, they all do.

hereiseeheresy No sulfide-ore copper mine in history has operated and closed without contaminating the surrounding water. Not one.
The Boundary Waters sits on the 1854 Ceded Territory of the Lake Superior Chippewa. The Grand Portage Band has formally opposed the mine.
